name: runtime-compile
_note: game-design.runtime-compile walks a level's layers + the game's entities into a deterministic, JSON-serialisable runtime scene: a bounded solid/hazard collision grid (from intgrid + collision config), a spawn point (player object instance, else first non-solid cell), placed actors, and the advertised mechanics. LENS_ACTIONS handler, 3-arg params path, in-memory per-user. Wrapped in try/catch → returns {ok:false, error:'handler_error'} on any throw, never crashes the runner. Gravity is clamped to [0,4000]; the grid is cols*rows where cols/rows were themselves clamped to [4,64] at level-create, so no runaway allocation is reachable. An unknown level rejects 'level not found'. No DB / LLM / network. Not driven by the assassin (LENS_ACTIONS, no derived base); pinned by server/tests/game-design-lens-macros.test.js (collision grid + spawn derivation).
